name: Mali

colors.symbolism: the country's mineral wealth, especially gold; purity
the blood shed in the struggle against French colonial rule

description: Three equal vertical stripes — green, yellow, and red.
history: Adopted on 1 March 1961, replacing the flag of the short-lived Mali Federation (1959–1960) which had borne a black human figure (kanaga) at the centre of the yellow stripe. The kanaga was removed in 1961 under pressure from Islamic conservatives who objected to representations of the human form. The colour order is the reverse of Guinea's flag (which uses the same Pan-African palette in red-yellow-green).

features.symbolism: the Pan-African colours adopted from the flag of Ghana, shared with several West African neighbours
